21.correct: Article 360 of the Indian Constitution deals with the proclamation of a Financial Emergency.

wrong.A: Article 352 deals with a National Emergency.
wrong.B: Article 356 deals with President's Rule in a state.
wrong.D: Article 365 deals with the effect of failure to comply with Union directions.

21.fact: A Financial Emergency under Article 360 has never been imposed in India.

22.correct: The 42nd Amendment (1976) shifted Education from the State List to the Concurrent List, allowing both the Centre and states to legislate on it.

wrong.B: The 44th Amendment (1978) reversed several Emergency-era changes.
wrong.C: The 52nd Amendment (1985) added the anti-defection law.
wrong.D: The 86th Amendment (2002) made education a fundamental right (Article 21A).

22.fact: The 42nd Amendment is called the 'Mini-Constitution' because of its sweeping changes.

23.correct: The 13th Finance Commission was the first to recommend a mechanism for compensating states for revenue loss on account of the move towards GST.

wrong.A: The 12th Finance Commission predates the GST compensation discussion.
wrong.C: The 14th Finance Commission came after the 13th.
wrong.D: The 15th Finance Commission is the most recent, well after the first recommendation.

23.fact: GST was finally rolled out in 2017, with a separate GST (Compensation to States) Act.

24.correct: Mulk Raj Anand's novel 'Two Leaves and a Bud' is based on the exploitation of workers in the tea plantations (of Assam).

wrong.A: It is not about textile-mill workers.
wrong.C: It is not about coal-mine workers.
wrong.D: It is not set among other labourers.

24.fact: Mulk Raj Anand was a pioneer of socially conscious Indian English fiction, also known for 'Untouchable' and 'Coolie'.

25.correct: The Human Development Report is the publication directly concerned with human development; it introduced the Human Development Index.

wrong.A: The Global Risks Report (WEF) deals with global risks.
wrong.C: The World Economic Outlook (IMF) covers growth and economic prospects.
wrong.D: The Global Competitiveness Report ranks economies on competitiveness.

25.fact: The Human Development Report has been published by the UNDP since 1990.

26.correct: The Energy Transition Index (Global Energy Transition Index) is published by the World Economic Forum (WEF).

wrong.A: The International Energy Agency publishes other energy reports, not this index.
wrong.B: The World Bank does not publish the Energy Transition Index.
wrong.D: OPEC is an oil producers' group, not the publisher.

26.fact: The index measures how ready countries are to shift to clean, sustainable energy systems.

27.correct: A standard football (soccer) match is played for 90 minutes of official time, split into two halves of 45 minutes each (excluding added/injury time).

wrong.A: 80 minutes is the duration of a rugby match, not football.
wrong.C: 100 minutes is not the standard football duration.
wrong.D: 120 minutes applies only when extra time is added in knockout matches.

27.fact: The 90 minutes is divided into two 45-minute halves with a 15-minute interval.

28.correct: The hardness of bone is mainly due to the presence of calcium and phosphorus compounds (calcium phosphate) in its matrix.

wrong.A: Iron and magnesium compounds are not the main hardening minerals of bone.
wrong.C: Sodium and potassium are electrolytes, not bone-hardening minerals.
wrong.D: Sulphur and chloride compounds do not give bone its hardness.

28.fact: Bone combines a hard mineral (hydroxyapatite) with flexible collagen fibres.

29.correct: At the Goa 2023 edition of the National Games, more than 20 athletes failed doping tests - one of the largest such numbers in the country.

wrong.A: Gujarat 2022 is not the edition with this record number of failures.
wrong.B: The Kerala edition is not the one referred to here.
wrong.D: Uttarakhand 2025 is not the edition cited.

29.fact: India's National Anti-Doping Agency (NADA) conducts dope testing at the National Games.

30.correct: A major aim of nationalising the Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) in 1956 was to mobilise people's savings to fund the country's planned development.

wrong.A: Promoting rural employment was not the primary aim.
wrong.B: Developing agriculture was not the direct purpose.
wrong.C: Regulating foreign trade was unrelated to LIC's nationalization.

30.fact: LIC was created in 1956 by merging many private insurers into a single state-owned corporation.

31.correct: Shankardev's teachings (Ekasarana Dharma) drew mainly on both the Bhagavad Gita (I) and the Bhagavata Purana (II).

wrong.A: 'Only I' leaves out the Bhagavata Purana.
wrong.B: 'Only II' leaves out the Bhagavad Gita.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both texts influenced him.

31.fact: Srimanta Shankardev was a 15th-16th century Assamese saint who founded the Neo-Vaishnavite movement.

32.correct: The SHRESHTHA scheme provides residential (boarding-school) education for meritorious students from Scheduled Caste families in reputed private schools.

wrong.B: The UDAAN scheme focuses on skilling/employment (e.g. for J&K youth).
wrong.C: PM-KUSUM supports solar power for farmers.
wrong.D: FAME India supports electric and hybrid vehicles.

32.fact: SHRESHTHA stands for 'Scheme for Residential Education for Students in High Schools in Targeted Areas'.

33.correct: The word 'Upanishad' literally means 'to sit down near' (a teacher) - referring to disciples sitting close to a guru to receive secret knowledge.

wrong.A: It does not mean speaking loudly.
wrong.B: It does not mean writing in good handwriting.
wrong.C: It does not mean meditating silently, though learning was reflective.

33.fact: The Upanishads form the concluding part of the Vedas and are known as 'Vedanta'.

34.correct: The 'Majuli Festival' is celebrated in Assam, on and around the river island of Majuli.

wrong.A: Goa is not where the Majuli Festival is held.
wrong.C: Karnataka is not associated with Majuli.

34.fact: Majuli, on the Brahmaputra, is the world's largest river island and a centre of Assamese Satra culture.

35.correct: The Zoological Survey of India (ZSI), founded in 1916, celebrated its 110th Foundation Day on 30 June 2025.

wrong.B: 90 years would place its founding in the 1930s, which is incorrect.
wrong.C: 99 is not the milestone marked in 2025.
wrong.D: 115 would imply a founding around 1910, which is wrong.

35.fact: The ZSI, headquartered in Kolkata, surveys and documents India's faunal diversity.

36.correct: Both rapid combustion (I) and spontaneous combustion (II) are recognised types of combustion, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are types of combustion.

36.fact: Other types include slow combustion and explosive combustion.

37.correct: The Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA) was founded at a meeting held at the Ferozshah Kotla ground in Delhi (1928).

wrong.A: The Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay hosted the first Congress session (1885), not the HSRA founding.
wrong.C: Alfred Park, Allahabad is where Chandrashekhar Azad died, not where HSRA was founded.
wrong.D: Jallianwala Bagh, Amritsar is associated with the 1919 massacre.

37.fact: The HSRA's members included Bhagat Singh, Chandrashekhar Azad and Sukhdev.

38.correct: According to Ministry of Health and Family Welfare data, India's Total Fertility Rate (TFR) stabilised at about 2.0 around 2021 - close to the replacement level.

wrong.B: 3.0 is higher than India's current fertility rate.

38.fact: The replacement-level fertility rate is about 2.1 children per woman.

39.correct: In the Indian budget, a subsidy is classified under revenue expenditure, since it does not create an asset and is a recurring transfer.

wrong.B: Acquiring fighter aircraft is capital expenditure (it creates an asset).
wrong.C: Acquiring a naval ship is capital expenditure.
wrong.D: Expressway construction is capital expenditure (asset creation).

39.fact: Revenue expenditure is for the normal running of government and does not create assets; capital expenditure builds or buys assets.

40.correct: The Quad Leaders' Summit 2025 is scheduled to be hosted in India.

wrong.A: The USA hosted an earlier Quad summit, not the 2025 one.
wrong.D: Japan hosted a previous summit, not 2025.

40.fact: The Quad is a grouping of India, the USA, Japan and Australia for Indo-Pacific cooperation.
